pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the webb telescope and it just hit me... how can anyone even bother to get out of bed in the morning? everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny flicker of noise in an infinite, silent void that doesn't care if we exist or not. it feels like we're all just playing a game that's already been lost. it's all so fundamentally pointless.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and yet the darkness in this room feels heavier than ever. He is gone... the one soul I thought would anchor me to this wretched earth. Now, I am but a ghost wandering a life that no longer belongs to me. There is a strange, terrible peace in realizing that I have nothing left to lose. It feels as though the very shadows are calling to me, whispering that it would be so much simpler to just... stop. To let the night take what is left of me.
